name: sandboxed_multi_turn_state
description: Proves the worktree mount persists across turn boundaries inside the sandbox. Turn 0 asks the agent to write a fact into a file; Turn 1 starts a fresh container (each turn is a separate `docker run`) and asks the agent to read it back. A green run proves the SAME workspace bind survives between containers -- multi-turn scenarios that build state across turns (e.g. `agent fixes bug, then writes test that exercises the fix`) depend on this. The pure-provider equivalent is `test_e2e_artifacts_written_in_one_run_visible_to_next_run_in_same_workspace`; this scenario covers the same invariant with a real agent in the loop.

turns.message:
Create a file named sandbox_state.txt at the workspace root with exactly this single line: persisted-across-turns
Do not modify any other file. Reply with just the literal word DONE.
Read the file sandbox_state.txt at the workspace root and reply with just its contents on a single line, with no extra prose, no quotes, no formatting.
